title: Living in Ballwin, MO — Complete Guide
content:
Ballwin ranks #1 small city in greater STL (KMOX). Homes average 8 days on market vs 40 national. Cost of living 6.9% below national average. 2nd hottest zip code in Midwest/Northeast (Realtor.com). Population ~31,103 on 8.99 square miles.

Safety: 84% of residents report visible/responsive police (Niche.com). Top 50 safest U.S. cities (Visual Capitalist).

Commute Times: Downtown STL 29 min, Forest Park 23 min, Historic St. Charles 31 min, Clayton 24 min, Chesterfield 10 min, Webster Groves 25 min. Note: Manchester Road congested during rush — use Clayton Road, Baxter Road, Kehrs Mill Road.

Real Estate: Median home price: ,000. Median household income: ,647. No city real estate tax. County/school taxes ~6.95% assessed value (~,200/yr on K home). Strong seller's market.

Schools: Parkway and Rockwood districts, both accredited with distinction. Parkway West High School. Nearby private: John Burroughs (top MO private high school). Higher ed: Lindenwood, Webster, SLU, WashU, STLCC.

Parks & Recreation: Vlasis Park (31 acres, athletic fields, fishing). Annual Ballwin Days in August (60,000+ visitors). Castlewood State Park (hiking, biking along Meramec River). The Pointe at Ballwin Commons (gyms, pool, fitness, pickleball). North Pointe Family Aquatic Center (lazy river, splash pad, water slides). Ballwin Golf Course (premier public, hosts Gateway PGA Junior Championship).

Dining: Marcella's Mia Sorella (Italian), The Wolf Cafe (healthy + live music), Bones French Quarter (Cajun), Clancy's Irish Pub. Painted Tree Boutiques for shopping.

Healthcare: Mercy Hospital, St. Luke, Barnes-Jewish West County. 47 retirement communities (6 with U.S. News recognition).
